title: Test and verify Phase 1
description: Verify buck2 --help works, toolchains cell generated correctly, wrapper injects config.
close_reason: Phase 1 verified: toolchains cell generated with go/genrule, wrapper script injects config for supported commands

title: Auto-regenerate Go deps on go.mod/go.sum changes
description:
Automatically regenerate the Go deps registry when go.mod or go.sum changes.

Approaches to consider:

1. **Pre-commit hook**: Run generator before commits that touch go.mod/go.sum
2. **Nix-native IFD**: Use import-from-derivation to parse go.mod/go.sum at eval time
3. **devenv enterShell hook**: Check if registry is stale and regenerate
4. **File watcher**: Background process that watches for changes

Considerations:
- IFD is powerful but can slow evaluation and cause issues with flake pure mode
- Pre-commit hooks require developer discipline
- enterShell hooks run on every shell entry (may be slow)
- Should warn if go.mod/go.sum is newer than generated registry

Recommended: Start with pre-commit hook + staleness check in enterShell.

title: Fix godeps cell indentation in .buckconfig
description:
The godeps cell entry in the generated .buckconfig is not properly indented. It should align with other cell entries under [cells] section.

Current:
```
[cells]
    root = .
    toolchains = .turnkey/toolchains
    prelude = prelude
    none = none
godeps = .turnkey/godeps
```

Expected:
```
[cells]
    root = .
    toolchains = .turnkey/toolchains
    prelude = prelude
    none = none
    godeps = .turnkey/godeps
```

title: Phase 5: Sync & Maintenance
description: Keep BUCK files synchronized with native project changes. Watch mode, CI checks, diff tool.

title: Implicit runtime dependencies for Buck2 toolchains
description:
Currently users must explicitly declare clang in toolchain.toml for Go builds to work, because Buck2's cxx toolchain needs clang in PATH during action execution.

Ideally, the mappings.nix should distinguish between:
1. Buck2 toolchain rules (go, cxx, python, genrule) - generate targets in toolchains cell
2. Runtime dependencies (clang, python3) - packages needed in PATH for Buck2 actions

When a user declares `go = {}`, turnkey should automatically ensure clang is available in the devenv shell without requiring explicit declaration.

Proposed solution:
- Add `runtimeDependencies` field to mappings.nix entries
- Have buck2.nix collect these and add them to devenv packages
- Example: cxx mapping would have `runtimeDependencies = [ "clang" ]`

title: Go external deps: simple case (no transitive)
description:
Add a toy Go project that imports an external dependency (e.g., golang.org/x/text or similar).

Requirements:
- Code must build with native `go build` (no import rewriting)
- When building with Buck2, deps loaded from Nix-managed cell
- Start with a dependency that has no transitive dependencies

Approach:
1. Create examples/hello-deps/ with a go.mod and main.go importing one external pkg
2. Generate a deps cell in Nix store with pre-built Go packages
3. Configure Buck2 to resolve imports from the deps cell
4. Verify both `go build` and `buck2 build` work
close_reason: Implemented: go build and buck2 build both work for hello-deps example with external github.com/google/uuid dependency

title: Design Nix-managed Go deps cell
description:
Design the architecture for a Nix-managed cell containing pre-built Go dependencies.

Key questions:
- How to structure the cell (one BUCK file per package? flat structure?)
- How to generate go_library targets from Nix derivations
- How to map Go import paths to Buck2 targets
- How Buck2 resolves `deps = ["//deps:golang.org/x/text"]` style references

Consider:
- Cell should be generated from go.mod/go.sum
- Nix builds the Go packages, Buck2 just references them
- Import paths must match what Go code expects

title: Go external deps: transitive dependency graphs
description:
Extend Go deps cell to handle transitive dependencies.

After simple case works (turnkey-538), add support for:
- Dependencies with their own dependencies
- Diamond dependency patterns (A->B->D, A->C->D)
- Version conflict resolution (use go.sum as source of truth)

Example: import a package like `github.com/spf13/cobra` which pulls in multiple transitive deps.

The Nix derivation should:
- Parse go.mod + go.sum for full dependency graph
- Build all transitive deps in correct order
- Generate BUCK targets with correct dep references

title: Phase 1 Exit Criteria (reference)
description:
Exit criteria for Phase 1: Toolchain Cell Generation (COMPLETED)

✅ Verified criteria:
1. `buck2 build //examples/hello:hello` succeeds
2. `buck2 run //examples/hello:hello` prints expected output
3. Toolchains cell auto-generated with correct Buck2 rules
4. .buckconfig symlink managed by devenv enterShell
5. .turnkey/toolchains symlink points to Nix store
6. Implicit dependencies resolved (go → python, cxx)
7. No manual .buckconfig editing required

Test: User declares `go = {}` in toolchain.toml, enables buck2, builds work.

title: Phase 2 Exit Criteria
description:
Exit criteria for Phase 2: Project Scaffolding

Required for completion:
1. `turnkey init` or equivalent generates complete Buck2 project structure
2. Prelude strategies all working:
   - bundled: Uses Buck2's built-in prelude (current default)
   - git: Clones prelude from git repo
   - nix: Uses Nix-managed prelude derivation
   - path: Uses local filesystem path
3. Generated .buckroot file marks project boundary
4. Root BUCK file generated (if needed)
5. Documentation for project initialization workflow

Test scenarios:
- New project: `nix flake init -t turnkey` creates working Buck2 project
- Existing project: Adding turnkey to flake.nix enables Buck2 builds

title: Phase 3 Exit Criteria
description:
Exit criteria for Phase 3: Go Project Integration

Required for completion:
1. Parse go.mod to discover Go packages in project
2. Generate BUCK files with:
   - go_library targets for library packages
   - go_binary targets for main packages
   - go_test targets for test files
3. External dependencies from Nix-managed deps cell:
   - Simple deps (no transitive) working
   - Transitive dependency graphs resolved
   - Code builds with both `go build` AND `buck2 build`
4. No import path rewriting required

Test scenarios:
- `examples/hello/` builds (already done in Phase 1)
- `examples/hello-deps/` with external dep builds
- Real-world Go project (e.g., small CLI tool) builds with Buck2

Depends on: turnkey-538, turnkey-539, turnkey-540

title: Phase 4 Exit Criteria
description:
Exit criteria for Phase 4: Multi-Language Support

Required for completion:

Rust support:
1. Parse Cargo.toml to discover crates
2. Generate rust_library, rust_binary, rust_test targets
3. External crates from Nix-managed cell (consider reindeer integration)
4. Code builds with both `cargo build` AND `buck2 build`

Python support:
1. Parse pyproject.toml to discover packages
2. Generate python_library, python_binary, python_test targets
3. External packages from Nix-managed cell
4. Code works with both `python` AND `buck2 run`

Test scenarios:
- Rust hello world with external crate (e.g., clap)
- Python hello world with external package (e.g., click)
- Mixed-language project (Go + Rust) in same repo

title: Phase 5 Exit Criteria
description:
Exit criteria for Phase 5: Sync & Maintenance

Required for completion:

Sync mechanism:
1. Detect when native project files change (go.mod, Cargo.toml, etc.)
2. Regenerate BUCK files automatically or on-demand
3. `turnkey sync` command to manually trigger regeneration

Watch mode:
1. `turnkey watch` monitors for changes
2. Automatically regenerates affected BUCK files
3. Integrates with devenv/direnv for seamless DX

CI integration:
1. `turnkey check` validates BUCK files are in sync
2. Non-zero exit if regeneration needed
3. Clear error messages showing what's out of sync

Diff tool:
1. `turnkey diff` shows what would change
2. Preview regeneration without applying

Test scenarios:
- Add new Go file → BUCK file updated
- Add new dependency to go.mod → deps cell regenerated
- CI fails if BUCK files out of sync with go.mod

title: Phase 4: Multi-Language Support
description: Extend generators to Rust (Cargo.toml), Python (pyproject.toml). Consider reindeer for Rust.

title: fix devenv module output
description: currently the turnkey devenv module is exposed as part of the flake.flakeModules output. This is confusing as flakeModules are for flake-parts modules, which this module is not. Instead, let's output it in a different flake.devenvModules output
close_reason: Separated module outputs: flakeModules.turnkey for flake-parts module, devenvModules.turnkey for devenv module. Updated CLAUDE.md documentation.

title: Extract Go deps into registry pattern
Phase 2: Project Scaffolding
description: Generate complete Buck2 project structure. Create scaffolding.nix, handle prelude strategies (bundled, git, nix).

title: Create nix/buck2/mappings.nix
description: Declarative mapping of toolchain names to Buck2 rules. Include go, rust, python, cxx, genrule. Mark non-Buck2 tools as skip.
close_reason: Created nix/buck2/mappings.nix with Go, Rust, Python, CXX, genrule mappings

title: Generate Go deps registry from go.mod/go.sum
description:
Create tooling to generate the Go deps registry from go.mod and go.sum files.

The generator should:
- Parse go.mod to extract direct dependencies and their versions
- Parse go.sum to get checksums for integrity verification
- Resolve transitive dependencies from go.sum entries
- Generate Nix expressions with correct fetchFromGitHub/fetchzip calls
- Handle module paths → repo URL mapping (e.g., github.com/foo/bar → GitHub fetch)

Output: A Nix file that can be imported as the Go deps registry.

Example workflow:
```bash
turnkey go-deps-generate ./go.mod ./go.sum > nix/go-deps-registry.nix
```

Or integrated into Nix evaluation via IFD (import from derivation).

title: Refactor buck2 wrapper into separate package
description:
Split the buck2 wrapper script into a separate Nix package to reduce inline bash in the Nix module.

The wrapper package should:
- Accept BUCK2_CONFIG_FILE environment variable as input
- Handle --config-file injection for supported subcommands (build, test, run, query, etc.)
- Pass through cleanly for unsupported subcommands
- Be a standalone derivation that wraps any buck2 binary

Consumer side (buck2.nix module) would then:
- Generate the buckconfig file as a derivation
- Set BUCK2_CONFIG_FILE env var pointing to the generated config
- Use the wrapper package instead of inline writeShellScriptBin

Benefits:
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Easier to test the wrapper independently
- Less inline bash in Nix code
- Reusable wrapper for other contexts
close_reason: Wrapper removed entirely - now using symlinks for .buckconfig and .turnkey/toolchains instead of wrapper script with --config-file injection

title: Support multiple godeps cells per repository
description:
Allow multiple Go dependency cells in a single repository, with BUCK targets specifying which cell to use.

Use case:
- Large monorepos with isolated Go projects that need different dependency versions
- Gradual migration where old and new code coexist with different deps
- Projects with conflicting transitive dependencies

Design ideas:

1. **Named godeps cells**: Instead of single `godeps`, support `godeps_frontend`, `godeps_backend`, etc.
   ```nix
   buck2.godeps = {
     default = ./go-deps-main.nix;
     legacy = ./go-deps-legacy.nix;
   };
   ```

2. **BUCK file marker**: Indicate which cell a package uses
   ```python
   # Option A: Target attribute
   go_binary(
       name = "myapp",
       godeps_cell = "legacy",  # or annotation
       deps = ["godeps_legacy//vendor/..."],
   )
   
   # Option B: Package-level marker file
   # .godeps file containing: legacy
   ```

3. **Generator awareness**: The go.mod→registry generator needs to know:
   - Which go.mod/go.sum to read
   - Which cell name to generate for
   - Could use directory structure: `go-modules/frontend/go.mod` → `godeps_frontend`

4. **Cell naming in .buckconfig**:
   ```ini
   [cells]
       godeps = .turnkey/godeps/default
       godeps_legacy = .turnkey/godeps/legacy
   ```

Considerations:
- Increases complexity significantly
- May conflict with monorepo standardization (turnkey-nnn)
- Could be future enhancement after single-cell pattern is solid

Recommendation: Design the single-cell implementation to not preclude this, but defer actual multi-cell support.

close_reason: Added import for buck2.nix in default.nix

title: Standardize on monorepo Go module layout
description:
Standardize on a single go.mod/go.sum at the repository root for consistency.

Rationale:
- Single source of truth for all Go dependencies
- Guarantees version consistency across all targets
- Simplifies Buck2 integration (one godeps cell)
- Aligns with Go workspace patterns

Implications:
- All Go code shares the same dependency versions
- examples/hello-deps would use root go.mod, not its own
- Need to document this as a project convention
- May need to support Go workspaces (go.work) for multi-module repos

Migration:
1. Move examples/hello-deps/go.mod content to root go.mod
2. Update examples/hello-deps to be a package, not a module
3. Update documentation

Alternative: Support Go workspaces (go.work) for repos that need multiple modules.
